title: Resource Limits in Sandboxes
concept: Capping CPU, memory, network access, and execution time to prevent runaway loops or resource starvation.

exercises.prompt: What is Docker used for in sandboxing?

options.text: To create temporary, isolated environments for code execution.
To increase the speed of the computer's CPU.
To connect the code to the internet more easily.

exercises.explain: Docker creates lightweight containers to run code safely, separating it from the host system.

cards.title: Why Resource Limits Matter
cards.body: Resource limits help ensure that a program doesn't use too many computer resources, which can slow down or crash the system.

cards.title: Resource Limits Defined
cards.body: Resource limits set boundaries on how much CPU, memory, and network access a program can use while running in a sandbox.

cards.title: Like Limiting Gym Time
cards.body: Think of resource limits as time limits in a gym: they ensure everyone gets a chance to use equipment and prevents long waits.

cards.title: Examples of Resource Limits
cards.body: Capping CPU time prevents a runaway loop from using all processor power. Limiting memory stops a program from taking all available RAM.

cards.title: Myth: No Limits Needed
cards.body: Some believe programs can run endlessly without limits, but this can cause system failures and slowdowns due to resource exhaustion.

cards.title: Visualizing Resource Limits
cards.body: Imagine resources like a bottle of water: limited amounts can be safely shared. Too much for one person can waste it for others.

left.text: Water bottle with a small cap.

exercises.prompt: What does capping execution time in a sandbox prevent?
exercises.explain: Capping execution time prevents programs from running indefinitely, which can mean they get stuck in loops.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Resource limits stop programs from using too much CPU, memory, and network access.
exercises.explain: True! Resource limits are important for maintaining system stability by controlling resource usage.

exercises.prompt: Resource limits help avoid ______________ in a sandbox environment.
exercises.sentence: Resource limits help avoid runaway loops in a sandbox environment.
exercises.explain: The correct answer is 'runaway loops', as limits are essential to prevent programs from misbehaving.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is an important reason to set CPU limits in a sandbox?

options.text: To ensure the code runs faster.
To prevent poor performance from one program affecting others.
To increase the number of programs that can run simultaneously.

exercises.explain: Setting CPU limits prevents one program from hogging resources, ensuring all programs run smoothly.

exercises.prompt: Why are resource limits crucial in sandbox environments?

options.text: They prevent a single program from using all resources, ensuring stability for others.
They allow programs to access more resources than normally available.
They eliminate the need for separate environments.
They increase the probability of system crashes.

exercises.explain: Resource limits are crucial to maintain overall system stability and performance, preventing any single program from consuming too many resources.
